FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class CaretBlinker

Namespace
FluentGpu.Animation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Animation.dll

Drives the focused editor's caret blink (the RepeatTicker phase-7 idiom): while an editable text node is focused, the CaretVisible bit toggles every half-period (Win32 GetCaretBlinkTime semantics — the host passes the OS value; headless uses the fixed 500ms default) and the node is marked PaintDirty so the recorder re-emits. One focused editor at a time (focus is singular). The host includes HasActive in its work gate so the loop keeps ticking at blink granularity while an editor is focused — and goes fully idle the moment it blurs. Zero alloc per tick (writes a POD row + a flag bit).

public sealed class CaretBlinker

object
CaretBlinker

Constructors

CaretBlinker(SceneStore) 

public CaretBlinker(SceneStore scene)

Parameters

scene SceneStore

Fields

DefaultBlinkMs

Default half-period (caret-on / caret-off time) — the Win32 GetCaretBlinkTime default.

public const float DefaultBlinkMs = 500

Field Value

float

Properties

HasActive

An editor is focused → the frame loop must keep ticking (at blink granularity).

public bool HasActive { get; }

Property Value

bool

Methods

Blur(NodeHandle)

Stop blinking for this editor (focus lost): caret hidden, Focused cleared.

public void Blur(NodeHandle textNode)

Parameters

textNode NodeHandle

BlurAll()

Blur whichever editor is blinking (window deactivated / overlay swallowed focus).

public void BlurAll()

Focus(NodeHandle, float)

Begin blinking for the (newly focused) editor's text node: caret shown, blink phase reset. blinkMs is the half-period (GetCaretBlinkTime); ≤ 0 falls back to the default.

public void Focus(NodeHandle textNode, float blinkMs = 500)

Parameters

textNode NodeHandle
blinkMs float

ResetBlink(NodeHandle)

An edit happened: the caret snaps visible and the blink phase restarts (WinUI/Win32 behavior — the caret never blinks away mid-typing).

public void ResetBlink(NodeHandle textNode)

Parameters

textNode NodeHandle

Tick(float)

Phase-7 tick: accumulate; on each elapsed half-period toggle the caret bit. A slow frame spanning several half-periods nets the parity (an even count is a visual no-op — no spurious repaint).

public void Tick(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
